pattern: Two lower-bound binary searches, one for the target and one for the value just above it, pin down the inclusive range of a value in logarithmic time.
hints: Because the array is non-decreasing, equal values form one contiguous block; you only need its two ends.
Finding the first index >= target gives the left end; finding the first index > target gives one past the right end.
Do two binary searches: the first occurrence is lower_bound(target); the last is lower_bound(target+1) - 1; if the target is absent return [-1, -1].

edge_inputs.description: target appears as a run in the middle

stress.description: non-decreasing array over a narrow value range so runs of the target are common
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(0, size)
    vals = sorted(rng.randint(-20, 20) for _ in range(n))
    target = rng.randint(-22, 22)
    return [vals, target]
stress.note: narrow range maximises duplicate runs to stress both endpoint searches
